title: Soren
content:
{{Character |image = Soren.png |real name = Soren{{Ref|CMC}}{{Ref|SI101}} |alias = {{Alias |undercover = [[Maria Hill]]{{Ref|SMFFH}}}} |species = [[Skrull]] |gender = Female |DOD = [[2024|Mid-2024]]-[[2026|Late 2026]]{{Ref|SI101}} |affiliation = [[Nick Fury's Crew]] |movie = ''[[Captain Marvel (film)|Captain Marvel]]''
''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]'' |tv series = ''[[Ms. Marvel (TV series)|Ms. Marvel]]'' (picture)[[:File:DODC Office 2.png|DODC Office Set Photo]]
''[[Secret Invasion (TV series)|Secret Invasion]]'' (1 episode) |docuseries = ''[[Legends]]''
*''[[Talos & the Skrulls]]'' |book = ''[[Captain Marvel: Beware the Flerken!]]''
''[[Captain Marvel (audiobook)|Captain Marvel]]'' |actor = [[Sharon Blynn]]
[[Kate Braithwaite]]
[[Charlotte Baker]] (disguised)
[[Cobie Smulders]] (disguised) |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|You got me?"
"I got ya.|[[Talos]] and Soren|Spider-Man: Far From Home}} '''Soren''' was a [[Skrull]] and the wife of [[Talos]]. During the [[Kree-Skrull War]], she hid inside [[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]] cloaked in Earth's orbit, along with her daughter [[G'iah]] and other Skrull refugees. In [[1995]], she was found by Talos, [[Carol Danvers]], [[Nick Fury]] and [[Maria Rambeau]] and was brought to [[Earth]] temporarily before leaving with the rest of her race to find a new home. In [[1997]], she was among a handful of Skrulls who returned to Earth in order to impersonate humans and aid Fury's spy career in exchange for Fury promising to find them a new home. In [[2024]], Soren and Talos were called upon by Fury, who decided to leave Earth and stay in space, to deliver [[Tony Stark]]'s [[E.D.I.T.H.]] glasses to [[Peter Parker]] on his behalf. Impersonating [[Maria Hill]] for the mission, Soren came into conflict with several [[Elementals]] and worked alongside Spider-Man and [[Mysterio]] before they learned of Mysterio's fraudulence. As a result of Fury's failure to deliver on his promise as well as his decision to stay in space, the Skrulls on Earth fractured into two divisions, with a [[Skrull Resistance|resistance]] group led by [[Gravik]] seeking to eradicate humanity and forcibly obtain Earth for themselves. Soren joined her husband in opposing the radical faction, which led to her being killed by Gravik. ==Biography== ===[[Kree-Skrull War]]=== ====Hiding inside Mar-Vell's Laboratory==== {{Quote|[[Mar-Vell]] warned us not to send a signal for any reason or the [[Kree]] would find us.|Soren to [[Talos]]|Captain Marvel (film)}} Soren's husband, [[Talos]], was a [[Skrull]] commander in the war against the [[Kree Empire]]. When their home planet of [[Skrullos]] was destroyed, Soren, her daughter [[G'iah]], and a number of other Skrull refugees escaped and wandered the universe in search of refuge from the Kree.{{Ref|CM}} Soren rescued a [[Santo Millika]] from Skrullos, that she would bring with her at all times.{{Ref|SI101}} By the year [[1989]], the refugees had found an ally in the rogue Kree scientist [[Mar-Vell]]. While Talos and his crew continued to search the universe for a new home planet, Soren, her child, and a group of other refugees moved in to [[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]] over [[Earth]]. However, the location was not made known to the other Skrulls before Mar-Vell was [[Assassination of Mar-Vell|assassinated]], causing Soren to lose contact with her husband. For the next six years, Soren and the other refugees lived as quietly as they could in Mar-Vell's lab, as Soren raised [[G'iah]]. Talos himself spent the next six years trying to find out what exactly happened to Mar-Vell with the hopes of reuniting with his family, serving as the General of the remaining Skrull forces fighting for survival.{{Ref|CM}} ====[[Battle at Mar-Vell's Laboratory]]==== [[File:Talso and Soren.png|{{AltFloat}}|Soren is finally reunited with [[Talos]]]] With help from [[Kree]] [[Starforce]] member [[Carol Danvers]], [[Maria Rambeau]], and [[Nick Fury]], [[Talos]] and Soren were finally reunited in [[1995]]. Unfortunately, the Starforce had also figured out their location. Starforce arrived and rounded up the Skrulls and planned on ejecting them into space despite them being civilians and unarmed non-combatants. [[File:Soren and Skrull.png|{{AltFloat}}|Soren covering [[G'iah]]'s eyes]] However, when Danvers unlocked her full powers, a battle broke out as the Skrulls and their human allies tried to escape the Kree. Most of the Skrulls were non-combatants, and Soren took measures to protect G'iah during the fight. Talos successfully impersonated a Kree soldier and pretended to round up the Skrulls, when in actuality he helped them load onto the [[Quadjet]]. Piloted by Rambeau, the ship fled the battle and returned to Earth. However, they were pursued by [[Minn-Erva]]. Eventually, Rambeau shot down Minn-Erva, after out-maneuvering her in an air battle. By the end of the battle, Danvers had successfully destroyed the Kree armada.{{Ref|CM}} ====Finding a New Home==== [[File:Skrull-Human Family Dinner.jpeg|{{AltFloat}}|Soren and her family have dinner at the [[Rambeau Residence]]]] {{Quote|I borrowed [[R. Keller|his]] likeness. I'm no thief."
"It is a little like stealing."
"Whose side are you on here?|[[Talos]] and Soren|Captain Marvel (film)}} Once the [[Kree]] were defeated, the [[Skrull]] refugees traveled to the [[Rambeau Residence]] for a meal with their rescuers. As they all had dinner, [[Talos]] told his family of everything he'd done to reunite with them, while Soren was taken aback that Talos stole a [[R. Keller|man]]'s identity. [[File:Talos&HisFam-EndScene.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Soren goes to find a new home with her family]] The Skrulls soon began discussing what to do now. [[Captain Marvel]] offered to serve as their protector as they traveled the universe in search of a new home planet. Soren and her family agreed to this proposition, and the Skrulls loaded into Mar-Vell's Laboratory and departed into space.{{Ref|CM}} ===Secret Spy on Earth=== ====Working for Nick Fury==== [[File:Soren (1997).png|{{AltFloat}}|Soren attends the secret meeting]] {{Quote|This is the promise. While you work to keep my home safe, [[Carol Danvers]] and I will find you a new one. Who'll be first to take the pledge? Thank you, Soren. You have a courageous mother, [[G'iah]].|[[Nick Fury]]|Promises}} Soren and the [[Skrulls]] were unsuccessful in finding a new planet for their people, so [[Talos]] and [[Nick Fury]] worked out a deal and organized a meeting for Skrulls who would like to flee to [[Earth]]. Soren and [[G'iah]] attended the meeting in which Fury promised to provide shelter on Earth while he and [[Carol Danvers]] sought a new home for the Skrulls, in exchange for the Skrulls serving as Fury's spy network. [[File:Soren Human Form.png|{{AltFloat}}|Soren agrees to help [[Nick Fury]]]] Assuming a human form in a show of allegiance, Soren was the first to step forwards and take the offer, an act Fury thanked her for, praising her bravery. The rest of the Skrulls then agreed to Fury's terms, and Fury assured them that he would keep his word.{{Ref|SI102}} ===Impersonating Maria Hill=== ====[[Attack on Ixtenco|Elemental Attacks]]==== [[File:SMFFH 4.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Soren and [[Talos]] first encountering [[Quentin Beck]]]] {{Quote|Nick, this was a [[Attack on Ixtenco|tragedy]], but it's not why we're [[Ixtenco|here]]. What, are we fighting the weather now?"
"Locals say the [[cyclone]] had a face."
"People say things when they're under stress. That does not mean that this is the start to some other big, world ending–|Soren and [[Talos]]|Spider-Man: Far From Home}} By [[2024]], Soren and [[Talos]] had returned to [[Earth]] to work with [[Nick Fury]] and [[Maria Hill]]. While Fury and Hill attended to other matters, Talos and Soren were tasked with impersonating them. Fury tasked them with making sure [[Tony Stark's Glasses]] and [[E.D.I.T.H.]] were given to [[Peter Parker]], revealing to them that he was Spider-Man. Eight months after the [[Blip]], they traveled to [[Ixtenco]], [[Mexico]], where a "[[cyclone]] with a face" had recently wrecked the small town. There, they encountered a [[Sandman (Elemental)|Sandman]] before immediately being shielded by a man named [[Quentin Beck]], who defeated Sandman. Beck would go on to explain that he was a superhero from an alternate dimension who had come to this version of Earth to protect it from the [[Elementals]] that were projected to strike in the coming weeks. [[File:Spider-ManFarFromHomeTeaserTrailer55.png|{{AltFloat}}|Soren and [[Talos]] face-off [[Sandman (Elemental)|Sandman]]]] While Beck showed a mastery of his abilities and managed to defeat Cyclone in [[Morocco]] days later, Talos and Soren thought it would be wise to find another superhero to help Beck out. Unfortunately, they were unaware of how to contact any other superheroes, causing them to recruit the only one they knew of: Peter Parker.{{Ref|SMFFH}} ====Recruiting Spider-Man==== [[File:SMFarFromHomeTrailer29.png|{{AltFloat}}|Soren being introduced to [[Spider-Man]]]] {{Quote|Parker, it's Hill. There's an earpiece with your [[Spider-Man Suit|suit]]. Put it on and await further instructions. Understood?"
"Uh... yes, ma'am.|Soren and [[Peter Parker]]|Spider-Man: Far From Home}} When [[Hydro-Man]] struck in [[Venice]], [[Italy]], where [[Peter Parker]] and his schoolmates happened to be visiting, Talos tracked Parker down and brought him to [[Nick Fury's Hideout]] to explained the situation to him. Talos introduced Soren as [[Maria Hill]] alongside the rest of [[Nick Fury's Crew]], and during [[Quentin Beck]]'s explanation she corroborated the location data he provided. When Spider-Man asked about any of the other [[Avengers]] perhaps dealing with the issue rather than him, Soren replied that [[Doctor Strange]] was unavailable. While Parker initially declined, Talos re-routed the school trip to the projected location of the fourth [[Elementals|Elemental]] [[Molten Man]] in [[Prague]]. As Talos later prepped Spider-Man for the elemental when they reconvened, Soren relayed that the city would still not evacuate, to Talos' distress. During the battle, when Beck and Parker were struggling to suppress the creature, Talos and Soren in their respective forms raced with their gear equipped to reach the duo. They arrived at the climax of the battle, where just as the Molten Man was gaining too much mass, and Beck seemingly sacrificed himself to destroy it before he emerged victoriously.{{Ref|SMFFH}} ====[[Battle of London]]==== [[File:Soren Hill and Talos Fury.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Soren and [[Talos]] prepare for the next attack]] {{Quote|Nick, satellites are picking up an EM pulse."
"I thought that was over."
"It's the biggest one yet.|Soren and [[Talos]]|Spider-Man: Far From Home}} When Soren detected an unexpected resurgence of Elemental activity, Talos called in [[Mysterio]] to scout the city of [[London]] to report what had happened. As the pulses increased with a [[EMP Generator]], Soren detected them, and said that the elemental was much larger. Once Beck departed, Soren received a call from [[Happy Hogan]] with a coded message, alerting him of Beck's deception. As the [[Elemental Fusion]] attacked [[Tower Bridge]], Beck's report of the matter finally convinced Talos that Beck was lying. Soren then noticed [[Spider-Man]] falling in from above, and Talos told Soren to be ready for anything. Readying for a potential attempt on his life, Talos sent Soren to the top of the roof of [[The Shard]] where she was to supervise the battle from with a rocket launcher. When [[Spider-Man]] exposed the illusion of the [[Stark Industries Combat Drones|drones]], including the cloaked drone sent to assassinate Talos, he signaled to Soren, who replied by firing at the drone and destroying it with the rocket launcher. Soren and Talos met with Hogan after the battle, and Talos tried to claim that he was suspicious of Beck all along; however, Soren called him out, saying he had zero doubts. Talos asked Hogan where Spider-Man was, and Hogan told Talos he would call him, which prompted a smile from Soren, and Talos said he had better, threatening Hogan.{{Ref|SMFFH}} ====Contacting Nick Fury==== [[File:Skrulls (Spider-Man Far From Home).png|{{AltFloat}}|Soren and [[Talos]] resuming their true forms]] {{Quote|You gotta tell [[Nick Fury|him]]."
"It was fine. The [[Spider-Man|little boy]] handled it. We helped."
"Talos.|Soren and [[Talos]]|Spider-Man: Far From Home}} Around a week later, Soren called Talos out for letting the situation get so far out of hand and ordered him to call [[Nick Fury]] about Mysterio's deception. Talos was initially adamant that everything worked out fine, but he eventually heeded to Soren's words and concluded his message with a request that Fury return to Earth.{{Ref|SMFFH}} ===Death and Legacy=== {{Quote|I understand. You're taking a risk."
"A risk? Is that what you explained to Mum, that following you could get her killed?"
"Your mother died while you were working for her [[Gravik|killer]].|[[Talos]] and [[G'iah]]|Resurrection (Secret Invasion)}} By 2026, the [[Skrull]] community hiding on Earth were split into two factions, one of which was led by Talos who adopted a pacifist approach to humans and the other by the extremist Gravik who sought to exterminate humanity and re-establish Earth as the Skrulls’ new home New Skrullos. The rising popularity of Gravik eventually caused [[Talos]] to be kicked off of the [[Skrull Council]], and in the chaos, [[Gravik]] became a member of the council. Although Soren stayed with her husband all along, their daughter, [[G'iah]], joined Gravik's cause. Soren was eventually killed by Gravik without G'iah's knowledge. Talos, however, would eventually tell Gi'ah, which would cause her to gradually doubt Gravik's cause. Before she died, Soren had planted a [[Santo Millika]], which adapted to [[Earth]]'s soil. Talos would continue to take care of the plant, and in [[2026]], would show the plant to [[Nick Fury]] when he visited.{{Ref|SI101}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== *'''Skrull Physiology''': Soren was a [[Skrull]] allowing her to possess superhuman abilities, most notably shapeshifting. **'''Superhuman Strength''': As a Skrull, Soren possessed superhuman strength. **'''Longevity''': ''To be added'' **'''Shapeshifting''': Like the rest of her species, Soren had the ability to shapeshift into sentient humanoid beings, perfectly matching their height, voice, unique physical characteristics, and even copying their clothing by way of [[Skrull]] co-morphing apparel. However, she had to visually perceive the person who is going to be imitated. She was able to shapeshift into [[Maria Hill]]. ***'''Vocal Replication''': Soren was able to perfectly replicate [[Maria Hill]]'s voice while shapeshifting into her. ***'''Memory Replication''': Soren was able to replicate many of [[Maria Hill]]'s recent memories. **'''Radiation Immunity''': As a Skrull, Soren possessed an immunity to radiation. ===Abilities=== *'''Expert Marksman''': Soren was an excellent marksman being able to shoot down an [[E.D.I.T.H.]] drone with a rocket launcher. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''{{WPS|Glock 17}}''': [[File:Soren RPG 2.png|thumb|250px|Soren destroys a [[Stark Industries Combat Drones|drone]] with a rocket launcher]] Soren carried a Glock 17 while disguised as [[Maria Hill]], the same weapon that Hill would carry. As she and [[Talos]] arrived in [[Ixtenco]], [[Mexico]], posing as Hill and [[Nick Fury]], she pulled out her sidearm when [[Mysterio]] appeared, but soon draw her aim and fired at [[Sandman (Elemental)|Sandman]], who emerged suddenly and [[Attack on Ixtenco|attacked them]]. *'''Rocket Launcher''': During the [[Battle of London]], she used a rocket launcher to destroy a [[Stark Industries Combat Drones|drone]] that was aiming at [[Talos]]. ==Facilities== *'''[[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]]''': Along with the group of Skrull refugees, Soren moved to their ally Mar-Vell's laboratory in the 80s where she lived for over six years hiding from the Kree. She fled the lab with the others when her [[Talos|husband]] came to retrieve them. *'''[[Rambeau Residence ]]''': Once they escaped from [[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]] and the [[Starforce]], Soren and her family were briefly hosted by [[Maria Rambeau]]. Knowing that they were in need of a safe, permanent place to live, the Skrull refugees were then taken to a new home by [[Carol Danvers]]. *'''[[Nick Fury's Hideout]]''': As a member of [[Nick Fury's Crew]], Soren had made base in Nick Fury's Hideout, where she debriefed [[Spider-Man]]. *'''[[The Shard]]''': Soren used the Shard along with [[Talos]] to communicate with [[Mysterio]], until the latter's deception had been revealed. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Talos]] † - Husband *[[G'iah]] - Daughter ===Allies=== *[[Mar-Vell]] † *[[Captain Marvel|Carol Danvers/Captain Marvel]] *[[Maria Rambeau]] † *[[Goose]] *[[Monica Rambeau]] *[[Varra]] *[[Nick Fury's Crew]] **[[Nick Fury]] - Friend **[[Maria Hill]] † **[[Dimitri Smerdyakov]] **[[Seamstress]] *[[Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] *[[Happy Hogan]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Kree Empire]] **[[Starforce]] ***[[Yon-Rogg]] ***[[Minn-Erva]] † ***[[Korath]] † ***[[Att-Lass]] ***[[Bron-Char]] *[[Elementals]] **[[Sandman (Elemental)|Sandman]] **[[Cyclone]] **[[Hydro-Man]] **[[Molten Man]] **[[Elemental Fusion]] *[[Quentin Beck's Crew]] **[[Mysterio|Quentin Beck/Mysterio]] † - Temporary Ally *[[United States Department of Damage Control]] *[[Skrull Resistance]] **[[Gravik]] † - Former Ally and Killer {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearance for Soren |- | {{Scroll box|content= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|CM}} **{{Appearance|CMBtF}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|CMA}} *{{Appearance|SI102}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|SMFFH}} *{{Appearance|MM1}} (photo)[[:File:DODC Office 2.png|DODC Office Set Photo]] *{{Appearance|SI101}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|SI102}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|SI103}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|SI105}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|SI106}} (mentioned) }} |} ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Sharon Blynn]] was uncredited for her appearance in ''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]''. *[[Cobie Smulders]] revealed in an interview with ''{{WPS|The Hollywood Reporter}}'' that she went through the entire production of ''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]'' under the impression that she was playing the real [[Maria Hill]]. The fact that Hill was Soren in disguise was revealed to her by [[Kevin Feige]] a week before the film's release. The footage of her and [[Samuel L. Jackson]] in the car just prior to the reveal was shot for another scene that ended up getting cut.[https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/video/cobie-smulders-spider-man-far-home-credit-reveal-watch-1228344 'Spider-Man: Far From Home' Star Cobie Smulders Weighs In on End-Credit Reveal | THR News] ==References== {{Gallery|Soren/Gallery}} {{Wq|Soren}} {{Reflist}} [[es:Soren]] [[ru:Сорен]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Captain Marvel (film) Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Far From Home Characters]] [[Category:Secret Invasion (TV series) Characters]] [[Category:Legends Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Skrulls]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:No Hair]] [[Category:Purple Eyes]] [[Category:Nick Fury's Crew Members]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]] [[Category:Characters Killed by Gravik]]
